item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad, not great. Neutral vote from me. Feels like I'm missing something so I'll probably change my mind when I reread it again later.

item.content:encoded:

Well, Minecraft SCP that somehow raises cosmic questions.

Also, Fortnite for April Fools

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:

Oh for God's sake, take your bloody upvote and go away.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This post radiates big dick energy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: don't you just hate it when you try to destroy some world but you get stuck in a video game and get force-fed string and rotten flesh and then you accidentally create Endermen

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The O5 Council made contact with Mojang AB, the current developers of Minecraft.

I…no. No. Stop.

item.title: Re: No
item.content:encoded: It simply rides on Minecraft popularity. Butthurt responses of minecraft players just confirm it.

item.title: Re: "Interactive" Tag
item.content:encoded: Agreed. From what I can tell, interactive SCPs require user input to achieve the full experience that an SCP has to offer. The map is entirely optional and does not play into the actual story, so it does not qualify.

item.title: Re: "Interactive" Tag
item.content:encoded:
The interactive tag is defined as

SCP or article has interactive elements that requires user input or interaction.

However, I'd argue that, since you don't particularly need the map to understand/read the article, then the tag isn't particularly necessary. At this point it's up to the author.

item.title: "Interactive" Tag
item.content:encoded:
Seeing as this SCP includes a world download, shouldn't it have the "interactive" tag?

I'm not a mod, just raising the question.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Something amusing yet incredibly cringeworthy came up regarding this SCP on the subreddit a couple days ago.

Apparently, the Italian Minecraft/SCP YT community found out about it and is now cranking out clickbait videos about 4335 invading their world. Which would already raise some eyebrows, but it seems they're also completely missing the point about 4335, instead just making it Herobrine 2: Electric Boogaloo, giving it eyes and a mouth, the ability to hold things, and making it more like a generic creepypasta than anything.

Exhibit A.

Which brings me to a relevant question: These YTers are not crediting the SCP Foundation in any way, shape, or form when making their videos, and this combined with their severe misinterpretation of SCPs in general is leading to the insinuation that it's just some Minecraft thing and not a website. Is this a violation of the licensing policy? (if this needs to be moved somewhere else I apologize, and will do so ASAP if prompted)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Y'know, as someone who is pretty knew to the Foundation, I thought at first that everyone takes it very seriously, but then I see articles like this and I'm dumbfounded as to how it managed to stay up. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really amusing, had a huge smile on my face the whole time thinking about a team of agents playing Minecraft and running around with SCP agent skins being super serious.

The monster itself is pretty interesting and as I write this out I just realized you wrote an SPC without redacting anything!
Congrats.

item.title: Map Easter Eggs
item.content:encoded:
I'm confused as to what's considered an easter egg. There's not a lot going on in the map that isn't already said in the article, and I only found two of them as well as one that I'm pretty sure was just a little bit of minecrafty humor.

Spoilers for my findings, I guess
It's not really special

The SCP-4521 reference is nice. I like the cute tree down there. Garfield is something I can see someone assigned to 4335 doing as a joke. The only other thing I found was the first incident entry where a certain specially named researcher created some questionable monuments outside. I spectator flew around everywhere else that made sense and found nothing. The attic would have been a perfect spot for something fun, but I was surprised to find nothing.

item.title: Re: No
item.content:encoded: you're fun

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I honestly thought this was gonna be Herobrine. Glad it wasn't.

I also really like the idea of Endermen being a result of an attempt to explain away the anomalous creatures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why not Mojang's Wikipedia page?
I'm a tiny bit belated on this but holy shit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm so happy Hatsune Miku created it all that time ago so we could have this amazing SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Even through all the stuff this game has gone through with the hatred, exploitation and milking by e-celebs, and being part of… shutters cringe culture… I'm glad that we can still recognize that minecraft is still a badass game for badasses. Happy birthday to min e crafte

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey all, it's Minecraft's 10th anniversary. Happy minecraft day, everyone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: hero brine real

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A Minecraft SCP should not work, but somehow this one does. Well done. Nice use of blending in real life elements too.

I should read things more carefully. Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Little nag, but when the guy mentions the Runescape SCP, he says that it was discovered three years ago, but according to the log, the MTF was deployed for this one in 2010, whereas the Runescape anomaly was found in 2013

Still a great article

item.title: Re: No
item.content:encoded: Found the party animal everyone

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i saw this in top rated and while the prior revisions are amazing, you must admit, arriving onto this page for the first time only to see the herobrine creepypasta was a bit w a c k

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, I don't know what I expected when I checked in on this on April Fools, but not this for certain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I updated the SCP. Hope y'all enjoy

item.title: No
item.content:encoded: This SCP has no sense of wonder, mystery or dread. I hope this is some kind of in-joke, that it is upvoted so highly.

item.title: Re: Inconsistency and inaccuracies
item.content:encoded: I think Plaguebearer brings up a great middle ground solution. Microsoft wasn't around for the start, but C418 was.

item.title: Re: Inconsistency and inaccuracies
item.content:encoded: again, make it link to C418

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All hail Microsoft.

item.title: Re: Inconsistency and inaccuracies
item.content:encoded: I totally understand, I really do. Good thing that wasn't (at least wasn't intended) to be the actual main punchline (which was that there is no God/creator in our universe). I'm glad you liked the article anyway!

item.title: Inconsistency and inaccuracies
item.content:encoded:
Don't get me wrong, I love this article; I personally think it's one of the best and well-known of Series 5. I think it's an awesome homage to one of my favourite games of all time. However, I couldn't help but note that, according to the article…

Several months after SCP-4335's first formal containment procedures were developed, on November 27, 2010…

But the stinger at the ends reveals that there is a god, named to be Microsoft itself, despite the fact that Mojang was bought by Microsoft (they didn't even make it, which makes this decision even stranger) in September 2014. I thought this was a little strange, honestly, until I dug through the page history and found out it was originally a hyperlink to Markus Persson (the actual creator of Minecraft)'s Wikipedia page.

I can't help but state my opinion that this is a rather unnecessary and hamfisted, and considering just how much controversy "Notch" has been of late, quite opinionated. I know that Notch has a bit of a tarnished image, but my final opinion is that you shouldn't have changed it to link to Microsoft's Wikipedia page.

Anyways, great article. I'm looking forward to more!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My reaction to the first dialogue with 4335 was something along the lines of "Yes! It's another sufficiently confused alien!"

+1

item.title: Re: Declassification!
item.content:encoded: It has my seal of approval.

item.title: Declassification!
item.content:encoded: Heya! I declassified this SCP over on r/SCPDeclassified. Check it out here or here for a better understanding!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: why didn't i think of that

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: On top of the criticisms previously mentioned, there was one thing that especially bothered me; why link to Microsoft's Wikipedia page? It'd be one thing if it wasn't obvious or alluded to, or on a tangential enough reference that it needed to be clarified out-of-universe. But you pretty much insinuate it hard there in that note. Linking to their wiki page is incredibly jarring.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

To point 1: So it can eat the containment chamber? Surely, once one tendril is finished consuming an item, it has to select another, right? Why is the containment chamber never targeted, especially if it's keeping the entity from accomplishing its objective?

It chooses the object that is closest to it. And technically, the items that fall into the containment chamber are closer than the obsidian walls.

If it's a cognitohazard that is for whatever reason tied in to, say, the movements the entity makes, that could be made clearer.

That, that's what I was going for. I'll attempt to make that a bit clearer.

To point 3: It's not clear at the time that the corrosion seen in the final log is a hallucination, as it's just stated matter of factly. This could be made more clear.

Fair.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block


To point 1: So it can eat the containment chamber? Surely, once one tendril is finished consuming an item, it has to select another, right? Why is the containment chamber never targeted, especially if it's keeping the entity from accomplishing its objective?

To point 2: It is mentioned three or four times that seeing the entity triggers the cognitohazard. If it's an effect only triggered by "actually" seeing the entity while playing, then it doesn't really fit the definition of a cognitohazard, because cognition isn't what triggers the effect. If it's a cognitohazard that is for whatever reason tied in to, say, the movements the entity makes, that could be made clearer. If it requires multiple sensory avenues simultaneously, then that opens up other plot holes (like, can you be immune by turning off your speakers).

To point 3: It's not clear at the time that the corrosion seen in the final log is a hallucination, as it's just stated matter of factly. This could be made more clear.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had originally intended to do so. But the sheer number of them made me give up halfway through. I shouldn't have to point out that many grammatical errors on a mainlist article, and I don't really have the energy to go back and find them. Tygently most likely got a good portion of them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, I see. Many thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

"The tendrils destroy all blocks but they're limited by obsidian, an exception that to my knowledge is never mentioned or explained."

That's partially my fault, but theres only a limited amount of tendrils, and all of them are occupied by the stuff that's constantly being fed to it.

"It's cognitohazardous to even glance at but there's just a straight-up unaltered picture of it."

It never says that pictures of it are also cognitohazardous.

"The images displayed by a literally dissolving monitor are somehow perfectly intelligible."

Well, the corroding doesn't happen to the hallucinating person instantly. I can probably clarify that, though.

Your other points have been taken into consideration, and when I have access to a computer, I'll see if I can somewhat mend them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not Bleep, but I made an edit earlier that fixed five grammatical issues I had noticed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agreed with all of this, and also to add my own few tidbits:

+ show block
– hide block

The Task Force segments are both ridiculous and clichéd. The novel nature of this concept should be an asset, but it goes basically unutilized throughout these segments because the characters behave in such an unbelievable, incompetent, and straight-up odd way.

The article is also filled with weird contradictions and elements that just don't make any sense. The tendrils are, in the same sentence, described as both stationary and moving. The tendrils destroy all blocks but they're limited by obsidian, an exception that to my knowledge is never mentioned or explained. It's cognitohazardous to even glance at but there's just a straight-up unaltered picture of it. The images displayed by a literally dissolving monitor are somehow perfectly intelligible. The Mobile Task Force dealing with a known cognitohazard doesn't take any precautions or reasonable actions, such as… turning off the computer when a task force member is in actual mortal danger. The cognitohazard exists but is basically never justified or tied in to anything, it's just another element thrown on top.

Elements are brought up and forgotten just as quickly. There's no consistent throughline because there are inconsistencies everywhere. Most egregious of all, the ending makes no sense given anything that preceded it; it reads like a melodramatic Note At The End only there to throw out unearned "wham lines" that don't make sense in the context of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I appreciate the critique

+ show block
– hide block

I.E. the use of obsidian as the material for the containment chamber, then stating TNT would be used to destroy the roof.

You misread it. TNT would blow up the IRON chamber that is above the actual obsidian chamber. I fully realize that a diamond pickaxe is needed to mine obsidian, I used to play the game a lot.

 (I'm looking at you containment procedure bullet points) 

That's… well that was a design choice, but if it is a bother, I can change it.

For example, Magma being a damaging block. By its very nature, it should be obvious that magma is a damaging block, and explaining it was entirely unnecessary,

That's fair.

All of your comments are totally understandable, but if it's not any trouble, what are the grammar issues you noticed? I've proofread this multiple times and I don't see anything that stick out that much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this a lot, it's a fun concept, well executed. You absolutely earned my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I originally set out to do a scathing line by line critique of this article. I gave up about halfway through the description. It's riddled with grammar issues, improper capitalization (I'm looking at you containment procedure bullet points) unnecessarily long-winded containment procedures that add nothing to the narrative intrigue, what little is actually there.

Show Summary Critique
Hide Summary Critique

The article falls on its face in so many areas it hurt to read through, failing to understand many of the mechanics of minecraft, (I.E. the use of obsidian as the material for the containment chamber, then stating TNT would be used to destroy the roof. Hint Hint, obsidian can only be broken by a diamond pickaxe in the base game. Not even the Wither or the Enderdragon can break it.) The article also fails to understand how minecraft multiplayer servers actually work (I.E. a server can be whitelisted so that only persons who have been whitelisted can access it. Why the hell would the Foundation not do this with a server containing an SCP.)

Many portions of the article explain things that don't need explaining, and then fail to explain other things that do. For example, Magma being a damaging block. By its very nature, it should be obvious that magma is a damaging block, and explaining it was entirely unnecessary, and made the containment procedures a crawl. On the other hand, ender pearls are thrown out in the containment procedures, and then not explained until the description. This is inconsistent presentation, and had everything been explained like magma, I would have had a more minor gripe, but as its not, it becomes more egregious for the things that are clearly unique to minecraft.

The dialogue is wooden, and demonstrates a lack of understanding on the front of how actual people speak. I had already hit points where I would have downvoted several times prior to this, but I would have downvoted on the dialogue alone.

I was never wooed by the concept, and it tread a totally predictable and entirely uninteresting narrative track which employed extremely poorly written storytelling measures.

I'm firmly of the opinion that something this unpolished, something this poorly written shouldn't have made it to the mainlist. This is a hard downvote. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I always thought something was up with endermen…

item.title: Re: +200
item.content:encoded: yey

item.title: Re: +200
item.content:encoded: MINECRAFT SCP

item.title: Re: +200
item.content:encoded: WOO! \o/
Happy to be the bearer of this upvote. Happy bicentennial!

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
The terrain issue was me having a lack of time and ability; if I wanted to terraform it to be like the picture, it would have taken hours, and every hill around the entrance would need to be modified to fit in nicely.

The MKA is put there with the assumption that no civilian should ever actually face off with the anomaly. The docs are there because they know that civilians always nose around where they shouldn't, and that if a civilian got on the monster probably obliterated the chamber anyways. Or at least, that's my headcanon.

The chicken farm was pretty much laziness on my part tbh. I knew that lighting up the area around the spawners would take way too much time, so I started brainstorming any possible idea that would generate enough blocks, and decided upon the chicken farm. Of course, this has its own downsides; putting in all the chickens via eggs takes a lot of time, so it's more convenient in some ways and less in others.

Thanks for checking it out!

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
It's a very believable, well-made map. Nice work bibliolatry!
Nice use of chickens instead of a mob grinder, definitely fits better space-wise and doesn't rely on lighting up the whole map. Using shulker boxes to carry documentation and emergency supplies was likewise smart.

That said, I'm not sure the MKA makes a lot of sense: if a random person stumbles onto this, they're given docs on the basics of what to do and a phone number to call. Would killing them really be that productive, they're facing off against a cognitohazard anyways.

Also I think it would be worthwhile to terraform the area right around the entrance to make it match the screenshot.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
brown bricks yes

(also, there are a couple of easter eggs. i wonder whomstdve finds them first?)

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Great news everyone! With the help of the amazing bibliolatry, a minecraft map based around Site-M1 has been created and is now available for download!

Some things I need to state:

  • The map was designed within Minecraft: Java Edition. We are not sure if it will work in any other version, such as bedrock edition or pocket edition.
  • Lag warning for the chambers themselves: don't expect perfect performance, especially if your system isn't that great.
  • If you want to preview the map without downloading it/or if you don't own minecraft, here's an imgur album showing multiple parts of the map.
  • Many, many easter eggs.
Licensing Info
– hide block

Licensing and Attribution info for "Site-M1" Minecraft Map

This work is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 3.0 (CC-BY-SA), and was created by Westrin and Bibliolatry/Technoturnovers.

Memetic Kill Agent #1 was created by Alexis Monnerot-Dumaine (https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Logistic_map_bifurcation_diagram.png), and is under the Public Domain.

Memetic Kill Agent #2 was created by Bibliolatry/Technoturnovers and is licensed under CC-BY-SA.

World Icon was created by TachankaOnWheels (https://old.reddit.com/r/SCP/comments/awgkjl/i_tried_making_my_own_mtf_unit_called_foxtrot1/)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't wait for the Scp readings with actual minecraft game play in the background to come out! +1!
